data.title: Silver Elephant Begins Silver Material Processing in Bolivia
data.description:
Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.

Silver Elephant Mining ( (TSE:ELEF) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Silver Elephant Mining Corp. has commenced trucking and toll milling of silver-mineralized material from its Apuradita Paca Project in Bolivia. The material is being processed at a facility in Potosi to produce silver-lead-zinc concentrate for sale. The company maintains a production forecast of 1,000 to 2,000 tonnes of silver-bearing material monthly and plans to toll-mill approximately 10,000 tonnes in the initial campaign. The discovery of high-grade silver-bearing veinlet systems during tunnel development presents additional exploration opportunities, potentially enhancing the company’s resource base.

Spark’s Take on TSE:ELEF Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:ELEF is a Underperform.

Silver Elephant Mining faces significant financial challenges with zero revenue and mounting losses, which heavily impact its overall stock score. Technical analysis shows some short-term stability but a longer-term bearish trend. Valuation concerns due to a negative P/E ratio and lack of dividends further weigh on the score. Mixed corporate events add complexity, with financial initiatives slightly balancing operational setbacks. Overall, the stock presents high risk with limited immediate upside.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:ELEF stock, click here.

data.title: AstraZeneca’s Promising AZD0120 Study for Refractory SLE: Market Insights
data.description:
AstraZeneca ((AZN)) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.

Study Overview: AstraZeneca is conducting a clinical study titled ‘A Phase 1b/2 Study of AZD0120, a Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-cell (CAR T) Therapy Targeting CD19 and B-cell Maturation Antigen (BCMA) in Subjects With Refractory Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE).’ The study aims to evaluate the safety, tolerability, and efficacy of AZD0120 in adults with refractory SLE, a significant autoimmune condition with limited treatment options.

Intervention/Treatment: The study tests AZD0120, a dual CAR T-cell therapy targeting CD19 and BCMA, designed to treat refractory SLE. Participants receive a single infusion of AZD0120 following lymphodepleting chemotherapy with Cyclophosphamide and Fludarabine.

Study Design: This is an interventional, single-arm, open-label, multi-center study. Participants are sequentially allocated to receive the treatment, with no masking involved. The primary purpose is to assess treatment effectiveness.

Study Timeline: The study began on March 12, 2025, with primary completion and estimated completion dates yet to be determined. The latest update was submitted on September 3, 2025, indicating ongoing recruitment.

Market Implications: This study could significantly impact AstraZeneca’s stock performance, as positive results may enhance investor confidence and market position in the autoimmune treatment sector. The development of effective SLE therapies is crucial, given the limited options available, and could position AstraZeneca ahead of competitors.

The study is currently ongoing, with further details accessible on the ClinicalTrials portal.

data.title: AbbVie’s Atogepant Study: A New Frontier in Pediatric Migraine Prevention
data.description:
Abbvie ((ABBV)) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.

Study Overview: AbbVie is conducting a Phase 3 clinical trial titled A Phase 3, Multicenter, 12-Week, Double Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of Atogepant for the Preventive Treatment of Chronic Migraine in Pediatric Subjects 12 to 17 Years of Age. The study aims to assess the safety and efficacy of Atogepant in preventing chronic migraines in adolescents, a significant step in addressing this debilitating condition in younger populations.

Intervention/Treatment: The study tests Atogepant, an oral tablet already approved for adult migraine prevention, against a placebo. The goal is to determine its effectiveness and safety in adolescents aged 12 to 17 years.

Study Design: This interventional study is randomized with a parallel assignment model. It employs a quadruple masking approach, meaning participants, care providers, investigators, and outcomes assessors are unaware of the treatment allocations. The primary purpose is treatment-focused.

Study Timeline: The study began on January 31, 2025, with primary completion expected within 12 weeks of the start date. The latest update was submitted on September 2, 2025, indicating ongoing recruitment and progress.

Market Implications: This study could significantly impact AbbVie’s stock performance and investor sentiment, particularly if Atogepant proves effective in a new demographic. Success may bolster AbbVie’s market position in the migraine treatment sector, potentially influencing competitors focused on similar therapeutic areas.

The study is ongoing, with further details available on the ClinicalTrials portal.

data.description:

CIMG ( (IMG) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On September 2, 2025, CIMG Inc., a Nevada-based company, received approval from the Nasdaq Hearings Panel to continue its listing on the Nasdaq Stock Market, contingent upon meeting specific compliance conditions. The decision followed a hearing on August 14, 2025, regarding the company’s adherence to Nasdaq’s listing rules. The company presented a compliance plan involving capital raises and new revenue contracts. The panel granted the request for continued listing, requiring CIMG to comply with specific rules by September 30, 2025, and to notify Nasdaq of any significant events affecting compliance.
